nodes.label: ADR 0007: Browser-native print, no server-side PDF renderer
nodes.rationale: Nearly every PRD module ends in a print-preview -> print/PDF flow. Three facts settled the decision: (1) Bangla needs full complex-text shaping that the browser's own engine (HarfBuzz) does perfectly but @react-pdf/renderer's fontkit shaping is unreliable, and headless Chromium would ship a ~50MB browser into a serverless function; (2) no PRD flow needs a PDF *file* — every printable is a browser-print/save preview, nothing is emailed or stored server-side; (3) the MVP fee receipt already prints this way successfully. Decision: printables are server-rendered Tailwind pages using window.print(), with a shared template layer in web/components/print/ (PrintPage, InstituteHeader, InfoGrid, SignatureRow, QrFooterRow, PrintButton) mirroring the legacy C_TAMPLATES shared-header/footer pattern. No PDF dependency added; zero serverless weight.

edges.source_location: Both ADRs reject a heavier/more isolated architecture (server-side PDF renderer; schema/database-per-tenant) primarily to stay within Vercel/Supabase free-tier cost constraints

hyperedges.label: Shared print-template layer across every printable module
